info.name: MarketPulse · Oxylabs + Application API
info.description:
Everything taught in class, runnable live.

Folder 1 hits the Oxylabs Web Scraper API directly (the data layer).
Folder 2 hits the MarketPulse backend (the agent with memory).

Setup: import the environment file next to this collection and fill in your Oxylabs credentials. Start the backend with `just run` before using folder 2.

item.name: 1 · Oxylabs Web Scraper API
item.description: One endpoint, many sources. Every request below is the same POST to realtime.oxylabs.io — only the `source` and `query` change. Auth is plain HTTP Basic with your Oxylabs API credentials.

request.description: The hello-world of the Web Scraper API: scrape an amazon.in search results page and get parsed JSON back. Look at results[0].content.results.organic — every product has asin, title, price, rating, reviews_count.
Full product page for one ASIN (boAt Airdopes 219): price, stock, rating distribution, sales rank, image URLs. autoselect_variant gives accurate buybox pricing for products with variations.
The bestseller list for a category. Same endpoint, different source value — that is the whole pattern.

item.name: 04 · Amazon pricing (all offers for an ASIN)

request.description: Every seller offering this product: who owns the buybox, who undercuts whom.
No dedicated parser? source=universal scrapes ANY public URL through the Oxylabs proxy network and returns raw HTML.

item.name: 2 · MarketPulse application API
item.description: The backend we built: a LangGraph agent behind FastAPI with Postgres checkpointer memory. Start it first: `just run` (or `just backend`). Run the requests top to bottom — the follow-up question only works because of short-term memory.

request.description: Is the backend alive, and is it scraping live or serving mock fixtures?

item.name: 02 · Chat — ask the agent (tool calls happen here)

request.description: One question in, and the agent decides to call search_products itself. The response carries the reply, the tool calls it made, structured product data, and the current memory stats. Takes 10-30s — the scraping is live.

'Of those' only makes sense if the agent remembers request 02. Same thread_id = same conversation, loaded from the Postgres checkpointer. Change the thread_id and ask again — it will have no idea what you mean. That is the whole lesson.
The agent picks the ASIN from conversation memory and calls download_product_images. The response's images[].paths are served by request 08.
Every conversation in the checkpointer — read straight from the Postgres checkpoints table.
The saved conversation. Restart the backend and call this again — identical. The Python process holds nothing.

Memory telemetry: message count vs the 12-message threshold, number of checkpoints saved, and the running summary once the summarization node has fired. Keep chatting past 12 messages and watch `summary` fill in.
Static serving of images the agent downloaded. Adjust the ASIN to match request 04's response (images[].paths).
